name: Vanilla-Sour Cream Bundt Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Generously grease and flour a 12-cup Bundt pan or tube pan. Tap out any excess flour.
2. In a medium bowl, using a fork, beat together the sour cream, milk, whole eggs, egg yolks, and vanilla until very well blended and smooth.
3. Add the butter and half the egg mixture to the dry ingredients. Beat at low speed just until thoroughly incorporated.
4. Increase the speed to high and beat for 1 minute; do not overmix.
5. Add the remaining egg mixture and beat at medium-high speed until the batter is fluffy and smooth, about 1 minute longer.
6. Scrape the batter into the prepared pan. Rap the pan on the counter several times to remove air bubbles.
7. Bake the cake in the middle of the oven for 50-60 minutes, or until it is well browned, pulls away from the pan sides, and a toothpick inserted in the thickest part comes out clean.
8. Run a thin knife around the pan edges to loosen the cake, then invert onto a serving plate.
9. Using a small strainer, sift a little powdered sugar over the cake if desired. The cake will keep for up to 5 days covered at room temperature or for up to 2 weeks wrapped airtight and frozen.
